Tomato Early Blight.organic_solution: Remove infected lower leaves immediately, improve airflow between plants, and spray diluted neem oil (5 ml per litre) every 5–7 days. Mulch the base to prevent soil splash.
Tomato Early Blight.chemical_solution: Apply a mancozeb-based fungicide (e.g., Dithane M-45) at 2 g/litre every 7–10 days. Alternate with chlorothalonil to prevent resistance.

Tomato Late Blight.organic_solution: Remove and destroy affected plant parts. Apply copper hydroxide spray (3 g/litre) every 5 days. Avoid overhead irrigation and ensure good drainage.
Tomato Late Blight.chemical_solution: Use a systemic fungicide containing metalaxyl + mancozeb (e.g., Ridomil Gold). Apply at first sign of symptoms and repeat every 7 days in humid conditions.

Tomato Leaf Mold.organic_solution: Increase ventilation and reduce humidity below 85%. Remove infected leaves. Spray potassium bicarbonate solution (10 g/litre) weekly.
Tomato Leaf Mold.chemical_solution: Apply chlorothalonil or a trifloxystrobin-based fungicide. Ensure thorough coverage of leaf undersides where the fungus sporulates.

Healthy Tomato.organic_solution: Plant looks healthy! Maintain mulching, balanced compost, and weekly scouting. Rotate crops each season to prevent soil-borne disease buildup.
Healthy Tomato.chemical_solution: No chemical treatment needed. Apply a preventive copper spray at the start of the rainy season as an optional precaution.

Banana Sigatoka.organic_solution: Remove and destroy infected leaves below the bunch. Improve drainage and airflow. Apply neem-based bio-fungicide and avoid waterlogging at the base.
Banana Sigatoka.chemical_solution: Use a propiconazole or mancozeb fungicide spray every 3–4 weeks. Alternate chemical groups to avoid fungicide resistance. Follow label doses carefully.

Banana Panama Disease.organic_solution: There is no effective cure once infected. Remove and destroy affected plants immediately. Disinfect tools with bleach solution. Replant with Cavendish or Fusarium-resistant varieties.
Banana Panama Disease.chemical_solution: No chemical cure exists for Panama Disease (Fusarium wilt). Soil solarisation and lime treatment can reduce fungal load before replanting. Consult an agronomist.

Healthy Banana.organic_solution: Plant looks healthy! Continue mulching with dry leaves, apply banana-specific compost, and monitor weekly for early pest signs.
Healthy Banana.chemical_solution: No chemical treatment required. Preventive foliar sprays of potassium-rich fertiliser can strengthen resistance during the rainy season.

Uncertain — please retake photo in bette.organic_solution: Capture a clearer image in natural daylight with the affected leaf filling the frame. Monitor the plant for 2–3 days and watch for symptom progression.
Uncertain — please retake photo in bette.chemical_solution: Do not apply chemicals until the disease is clearly identified. Consult a local agronomist or extension officer.

Unknown.organic_solution: Capture a clearer leaf image in good lighting and monitor the plant for 2–3 days.
Unknown.chemical_solution: Avoid spraying chemicals until symptoms are better identified. Consult a local agronomist.
